response:
The probability that a randomly selected student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A is approximately 0.6179.

We define the difference variable as D equals X_B minus X_A. Since both variables are normally distributed, the difference D is also normally distributed. The mean of D is 75 minus 70 which equals 5. The variance of D is the sum of the individual variances so 144 plus 100 equals 244. The standard deviation of D is the square root of 244 which is about 15.62. We calculate the Z score by dividing the mean difference by the standard deviation to get 5 divided by 15.62 which is roughly 0.32. The cumulative probability for this Z score gives the chance that D is greater than zero. This statistical process yields a result near 61.8 percent.

The performance improvement levels for both groups are equal at approximately time t equals 14.3. Group A follows an exponential growth path while Group B uses a logistic curve. An explanation of this intersection requires solving the equation where 50 times e to the power of 0.02t equals 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of negative 0.15 times t minus 5. There is nothing simple about solving this transcendental equation by hand so we rely on numerical methods. The force of the logistic model eventually slows growth while the exponential model continues to rise steadily. This mathematical process determines the specific moment when the two curves cross. The solution indicates that the groups match in performance improvement around the 14.3 mark of the semester timeline.
